problem.statement: Paid subscriptions and commercial benefits require one governed DSH orchestration and WLT financial lifecycle from plan publication through payment, activation, renewal, cancellation, expiry, compensation and client readback.
problem.currentWorkaround: The repository can display plans and create a payment-bound purchase, but the client surface has no purchase actions and the lifecycle does not govern renewal, cancellation, expiry, compensation or activation loyalty evidence end to end.

actors.role: Authenticated customer purchasing and consuming a paid commercial entitlement
permittedActions: read own effective benefits and lifecycle
activate a purchase after captured payment
renew an active subscription through new payment evidence
cancel an owned active subscription with a reason
forbiddenActions: read or mutate another client subscription
change price currency or WLT financial status
claim completed compensation without WLT evidence

permittedActions: create and edit subscription plan drafts
read WLT-backed active subscriber and recurring revenue summaries
inspect operational purchase and lifecycle diagnostics
forbiddenActions: mutate wallet ledger payment or refund truth in DSH
publish subscriber or revenue claims without WLT readback

actors.role: Authenticated DSH service caller orchestrating WLT-owned financial operations
permittedActions: request governed WLT activation renewal cancellation and expiry
read WLT client entitlement and compensation truth
persist non-financial operational projections and audit correlation
forbiddenActions: derive payment capture from a DSH flag
retry a mutation without an idempotency key

surfaces.exclusionReason: JRN-027 is a client paid-benefit lifecycle; partners neither own nor administer client subscriptions.
Captains have no paid client subscription entitlement or lifecycle action in JRN-027.
Field staff do not own client subscription plans, payments or entitlements in JRN-027.

outcome.statement: Every client subscription is backed by an exact WLT product and captured payment, exposes one effective entitlement, and follows an idempotent auditable lifecycle through activation, renewal, cancellation, expiry and compensation.
guardrailMetrics: subscriber or MRR claim without WLT evidence count
outcome.baselineState: Plans and basic purchase activation exist, but the client cannot operate them and lifecycle, expiry, renewal, cancellation, compensation and exactly-once loyalty evidence are incomplete.
outcome.targetState: Client and operator surfaces consume one DSH shared controller backed by WLT financial truth with complete lifecycle actions, negative invariants, readback and evidence.
outcome.observationWindow: targeted static, database, contract, backend, frontend, security, finance, runtime and independent product review

criteria: Only an active DSH plan with an exactly matching active WLT product is purchasable.
Purchase creation is tenant isolated, idempotent and resumable without orphaning a WLT payment session.
Activation fails before capture and succeeds exactly once after payment binding integrity passes.
Activation appends the configured WLT loyalty award exactly once.
Client benefits expose only effective unexpired WLT entitlements and legal allowed actions.
Renewal uses a new bound payment session and extends the period without overlap or double charge.
Cancellation immediately removes effective benefits or records a governed period-end cancellation according to policy.
Due subscriptions expire deterministically and no longer contribute to active subscriber or recurring revenue summaries.
Compensation state and completion reference are owned and read from WLT.
Control panel subscriber and MRR summaries are WLT backed and fail closed when unavailable.
All required controls are bound through the shared controller with loading conflict offline recovery and disabled busy states.
No runtime mock fixture local financial calculation or duplicate truth owner remains.
requiredChecks: DSH and WLT migration invariant tests
DSH and WLT route registration tests
OpenAPI contract and generated-client drift check
TypeScript shared marketing and app-client typecheck
renewal cancellation expiry and compensation integration tests

business: DSH owns marketing definitions and operational orchestration while WLT exclusively owns product amount, payment, subscription period, loyalty, compensation and recurring revenue truth.
Every financial mutation carries service authentication, tenant context, correlation and idempotency.
Every subscription purchase and lifecycle action is read back from WLT before the client sees success.
Every activation renewal cancellation expiry and compensation transition retains append-only lifecycle evidence.
Only one effective active paid entitlement exists per client.
negative: No DSH or frontend component writes wallet ledger payment refund or loyalty balance truth.
No captured payment activates a different client tenant product amount currency or DSH purchase.
No expired cancelled or uncompensated-as-complete subscription exposes paid benefits.
No idempotency key represents different mutation inputs.
No plan creator activates the same plan.
No subscriber or MRR number is computed from DSH purchase rows.

owners.productOwner: DSH and WLT Subscription Operations Product Owner

execution.appetite: Close JRN-027 code, data, contract, lifecycle, surface and verification gaps without duplicating WLT financial ownership, deploying production or activating commercial SaaS.
fixedConstraints: Repository mode is REMOTE_ONLY on sambassam.
WLT remains the sole owner of payment subscription loyalty compensation and recurring revenue truth.
DSH remains the operational orchestrator and marketing definition owner.
No PR merge force push release production deployment or commercial SaaS activation is authorized.
Independent product approvals cannot be self-issued by engineering.
variableScope: immediate versus period-end cancellation policy when explicitly represented in WLT
forbiddenScope: DSH-owned financial ledger or wallet truth
self-approval of product or visual acceptance
